steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined portion. The passage first establishes that any young playwright arriving in Madrid must go to Don Jerónimo to get work produced — he is a gatekeeper. The underlined portion then describes how he treats those playwrights: he receives them warmly but “never spared the young authors” and “criticized vigorously, even to the verge of insult.”

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It conveys Don Jerónimo's lack of interest in continuing to support playwrights.: The underlined portion shows the opposite — he engages actively and critically. ✗
  • Choice B — It explains Don Jerónimo's view of the quality of theatrical works of the time.: The focus is on his treatment of the young authors, not a general view of theatrical quality. ✗
  • Choice C — It creates a contrast between the talent of a young playwright and that of Don Jerónimo.: No such contrast exists — the passage never compares their respective talents. ✗
  • Choice D — It emphasizes the degree to which Don Jerónimo's support is consequential for inexperienced playwrights.: The passage has already established that he controls access to production; describing his harsh, rigorous criticism shows that his judgment truly matters — his support carries real consequences. ✓
That matches Choice D — It emphasizes the degree to which Don Jerónimo's support is consequential for inexperienced playwrights..
